articles.title: It’s Time to Bring Back Tsarukyan & Mokaev to the Octagon
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan vs Muhammad Mokaev wasn’t just a grappling match — it was a statement. Two of the best young fighters in the sport found a way to keep their names relevant without fighting in the UFC, which is incredibly difficult to do. Muhammad Mokaev was 14-0 and ranked #3 in the world when the UFC released him. The promotion said he was “difficult to deal with,” something Mokaev says caught him completely off guard. Instead of disappearing, Mokaev has done the opposite. He’s taken wrestling matches in Russia, fights in other promotions, and now stepped in on short notice to grapple Arman Tsarukyan — giving up nearly 30 pounds in the process. Tsarukyan deserves credit too. Taking a match where the public believes you're supposed to win is never easy. If you win, you get no credit. If you lose, you hear about it forever. The result was a fast, chaotic grappling match before Tsarukyan eventually locked up the choke. But the real story isn’t who won. The story is that Mokaev is still competing everywhere he can — and Chael believes that deserves recognition. Sometimes the hardest thing in combat sports isn’t winning fights. It’s staying relevant when you’re not fighting in the UFC. And right now, Mokaev is doing everything he can to prove he belongs back. articles.title: Sean O’Malley REVEALS what SHAPED him into becoming UFC world champion | The Daniel Cormier Show
articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by former UFC Bantamweight champion Suga Sean O’Malley, where he talks about his upbringing in Helena, Montana, and how that type of life shaped him into the fighter he is today. Sean grew up in a city with only 30,000 people, and said he played all types of sports growing up from football, basketball, baseball and soccer. He didn’t pick up fighting until he was a teenager, and he credits playing a bunch of sports when he was young for being such a fluid athlete in the UFC. Sean talks about meeting Tim Welch when he was 17 years old, and saving up money to go train at his gym in Arizona. Tim has become Sean’s longtime coach and mentor. articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. Every time a fighter left for the show, someone new would get in their ear. And almost none of them came back the same. articles.title: The Wildest Weight Class at 2026 NCAA Wrestling Tournament
articles.description: The NCAA wrestling brackets are out, and one weight class immediately stands out: 133 pounds. This bracket is loaded with talent, controversy, and one of the most fascinating stories of the tournament — Jax Forrest. Forrest transferred directly from high school to Oklahoma State mid-season, something rarely seen in college wrestling. Despite the unusual path, he enters the tournament as the #1 seed and the #1 ranked wrestler by USA Wrestling. But the road to the title is anything but easy. Last year’s NCAA finalists Drake Ayala and Lucas Byrd are sitting at the 6 and 7 seeds, while top contenders like Marcus Blaze, Davino, and Aaron Seidel are all lurking in the bracket. Chael breaks down why this weight class could produce some of the most unpredictable matches of the entire NCAA tournament, and why the freshman class may be about to take over. articles.title: The Night Francis Shocked the World #ufc
articles.description: I’m telling you flat out — that was one of the most remarkable sporting moments I’ve ever witnessed. Francis Ngannou stepped into a boxing ring with the lineal heavyweight king, Tyson Fury, and he didn’t just survive… he won. At least, he should have. One licensed judge scored it for Francis. A whole lot of fans scored it for Francis. Even Joe Rogan said he thought Francis won. And when that knockdown happened in Round 2? I jumped off the couch. That wasn’t luck. That wasn’t a gimmick. That was power, timing, and a fighter who refused to play the role of opponent. Let’s be clear about something: Francis wasn’t a novelty act crossing over. He was a former undisputed UFC heavyweight champion proving he belonged on the biggest stage in combat sports. So what’s the real question? Do you want to be “world champion”… Or do you want the biggest fights, the biggest moments, and the biggest paydays? Because Francis walked into enemy territory, shocked the world, and left with global respect. He changed how MMA fighters are viewed in boxing — overnight. That’s bigger than a belt. That’s legacy. And deep down, we all know what we saw. Watch it back. Tell me he didn’t win. #FrancisNgannou #TysonFury #Boxing #MMA #UFC #CombatSports #Heavyweight #FightNight #BoxingNews

articles.title: If Pereira Wins the Interim Belt… Does It Count?
articles.description: If Pereira Wins the Interim Belt… Does It Count? Is He Really Champ Champ Champ? If Alex Pereira wins at the UFC’s massive White House event, could he actually become champ-champ-champ? Chael Sonnen breaks down one of the most controversial topics in MMA: interim championship belts. Most fighters dismiss interim titles entirely. Some champions have even thrown the belt on the ground after winning it. But Chael argues something very different. History shows that interim belts often become undisputed belts. In fact, that exact scenario recently happened in the UFC heavyweight division when Tom Aspinall was elevated from interim champion to undisputed champion. So what happens if Pereira wins the interim heavyweight championship while already holding titles in other divisions? Does that count as three belts at once? Many fighters — including Conor McGregor and Henry Cejudo — don’t see interim belts the same way Chael does. But the UFC continues to create them for one reason: Because sometimes the interim champion becomes the real champion. articles.title: The Hard Truth About Jon Jones’ Future #ufc
articles.description: This might be the strangest position we’ve ever seen Jon Jones in. He’s 38. He’s still elite. But now he’s talking about arthritis. Hip issues. Physical limits. And suddenly, the conversation changes. It’s no longer about dominance. It’s about durability. For a guy who’s spent his entire career making the impossible look easy, this is a hard reality to watch. You can hear it in his tone. You can see it in the situation. Time is undefeated — even for legends. Behind the scenes, there were negotiations. There were asks. There were plans. But the division kept moving. A fighter Jones finished in minutes got a major opportunity. The belt picture shifted. And now we’re back to the scenario that was supposed to happen months ago: If Jon comes back, he likely has to fight for a belt. If he wins it, he defends it against Tom Aspinall. Full circle. It’s not glamorous. It’s not perfect. But it’s probably the cleanest solution. The question isn’t whether Jon still has greatness. The question is whether his body will let him chase it. And that’s a brutally human place for an all-time great to be. Does he push through one more run? Or protect what’s left and walk away? Legacy vs longevity. Greatness vs reality. Not an easy call — even for Jon Jones. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #TomAspinall #Heavyweight #UFCTitle #FightNews #CombatSports #GOATDebate

articles.title: Did Islam Makhachev Back Down From Ilia Topuria?
articles.description: Did Islam Makhachev turn down a fight with Ilia Topuria — again? Chael Sonnen dives into the growing debate surrounding the potential superfight between Islam Makhachev and Ilia Topuria. According to reports circulating in MMA media, Topuria accepted an offer to fight Islam — but the fight never materialized. Chael walks through the full timeline. One year ago, Topuria vacated the featherweight title and moved up to lightweight, aggressively campaigning for a fight with Makhachev. At the time, Khabib Nurmagomedov publicly pushed back, arguing that Topuria should earn a ranking at 155 before challenging the champion. Soon after, Islam himself vacated the lightweight title to move up to welterweight, preventing the matchup entirely. Now the story may have repeated itself. Topuria and his manager Malki Kawa claim that a fight with Islam was recently offered for the White House card and that Topuria immediately accepted. However, the fight reportedly fell apart — with explanations ranging from injury to financial negotiations. Chael explains why the situation raises an interesting question about legacy. If two dominant champions from the same era never meet in the cage, how will history decide who was truly the better fighter? And one key detail still hasn’t been answered: What weight class was the fight supposed to happen at? #UFC #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #ChaelSonnen #Khabib #MMA 0:00 Did Islam back down from Ilia Topuria? 0:15 Chael explains the controversy 0:38 Topuria vacates featherweight title 1:00 Ilia campaigns for the Islam fight 1:26 Charles Oliveira pushes back 1:45 Khabib explains why Islam wouldn’t fight Ilia 2:12 Islam vacates the belt and moves to 170 2:45 Comparing Islam and Ilia’s careers 3:24 Could Ilia have been a triple champion? 4:00 Why Chael favors Ilia in pound-for-pound debate 4:26 Islam responds to Chael’s criticism 4:49 Ilia claims he accepted the fight 5:20 Justin Gaethje’s White House card story 5:59 Would Ilia fight Islam at 170? 6:30 The Conor McGregor comparison 7:00 The unanswered question: what weight class? In this breakdown, Chael explains why the criticism surrounding the rumored White House event may be missing the bigger picture. According to Chael, fans often get caught up in fantasy matchmaking — imagining dream fights involving stars like Jon Jones, Conor McGregor, or Alex Pereira — without realizing how difficult it is to actually put those fights together. Chael points back to UFC 300, which many people initially criticized when the card was announced. But once the event unfolded, it delivered one of the greatest nights in UFC history, including the unforgettable Max Holloway vs Justin Gaethje finish and multiple dramatic moments that nobody predicted. That’s the key point. The UFC can only book the fighters who say yes. When the promotion puts together an event, they’re bringing the best matchups available at that moment. In the case of the White House card, Chael argues the promotion has already delivered three of the biggest stars in the sport — Sean O’Malley, Alex Pereira, and Ilia Topuria — plus Justin Gaethje, one of the most respected fighters in MMA. And once the fights actually happen, the real drama begins. articles.title: Francis Ngannou Beat the System...
articles.description: Would you rather be a world champion… or make millions of dollars? Chael Sonnen breaks down the incredible career arc of Francis Ngannou and why his story may be one of the most remarkable in combat sports history. Francis captured the UFC heavyweight championship, defeating Stipe Miocic to become the undisputed king of the division. But instead of staying put for the expected superfight with Jon Jones, Ngannou took a completely different path. Leaving the UFC, Francis bet on himself. Ngannou shocked the sports world when he secured a blockbuster boxing match against Tyson Fury—a fight most people believed would never happen. In one of the most surprising performances in combat sports, Ngannou dropped Fury and arguably did enough to win on the scorecards. The fight instantly turned him into a global attraction. From there, Ngannou landed another massive payday against Anthony Joshua, reportedly earning tens of millions of dollars. Add in his PFL deal, signing bonus, and guaranteed purses, and suddenly the narrative around his career looks very different. For years fighters have debated the ultimate question: Do you chase the belt, or do you chase the money? According to Chael, Francis Ngannou might be the rare fighter who did both. Watch the full breakdown as Chael explains why Ngannou’s gamble may have rewritten the playbook for fighters forever. #FrancisNgannou #UFC #ChaelSonnen #TysonFury #PFL #Boxing #MMA Francis Ngannou’s career story – 00:00 The champion vs money question – 00:15 Why younger fighters choose the belt – 00:28 Ngannou becomes UFC heavyweight champion – 00:51 The Jon Jones fight that never happened – 01:05 How Ngannou landed the Tyson Fury fight – 01:27 Why Chael calls Fury vs Ngannou historic – 01:48 Did Ngannou actually beat Tyson Fury? – 02:00 The Anthony Joshua payday – 02:20 Ngannou’s PFL deal explained – 02:35 Why promotions struggled working with Francis – 03:00 The revenue reality of big purses – 03:30 Jake Paul entering the Ngannou story – 04:05 Why Francis may have rewritten the playbook – 04:30 The final verdict on Ngannou’s gamble – 04:38 Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Ngannou Sends Message to Jon Jones Over UFC Contract Dispute
articles.description: Francis Ngannou weighed in on the contract situation surrounding Jon Jones and the UFC. Ngannou spoke about fighter freedom and the challenges athletes face when negotiating with promotions. Ngannou said fighters are private contractors and should be able to walk away if they aren’t getting what they deserve: “We should be able to get what we deserve… and if we don’t get it, we should have the right to walk away and look at other options.” He also discussed how contracts can tie fighters up and lead to situations where promotions may try to lowball them during negotiations. “There is always contracts tying people up… and then when you want to set free they massage you up and lowball you.” Ngannou added that he’s happy to be free from that system now. 👀 What do you think about Francis Ngannou’s message regarding fighter contracts? #FrancisNgannou #JonJones #UFC #MMA #FighterPay #MMAnews Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/
